name: Montgó and La Vall de Laguar Loop
name_original: Vuelta por el Montgó y La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Benissa
description: This difficult 57.7-mile road cycling loop around Montgó and La Vall de Laguar offers challenging climbs and stunning coastal views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Benissa offers diverse terrain, transitioning from a Mediterranean coastline with coves and beaches to rugged mountain ranges. The region features rolling hills, vineyards, and imposing peaks such as Sierra de Bernia and Sierra de Oltá. This varied topography provides a range of routes for different skill levels. Well-maintained roads and a cycle-friendly environment further enhance the experience for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Benissa The most popular road cycling route is View of Vall de Pop – Coll de Rates loop from Benissa, a 42.7 miles (68.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes the well-known Coll de Rates climb, offering significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Vall d'Ebo Pass – Pego–Sagra Road loop from Benissa, a difficult 48.3 miles (77.7 km) path. This route traverses inland passes and offers varied scenery through the region's interior. Local road cyclists also love the Montgó and La Vall de Laguar Loop, a 57.7 miles (92.8 km) trail leading through the prominent Montgó massif, often completed in about 4 hours 40 minutes. Road cycling around Benissa is defined by its Mediterranean coastline, rugged mountain ranges, and serene vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to more moderate coastal rides. The routes in Benissa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 13,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Benissa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Benissa?
A: Benissa offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find routes winding through picturesque vineyards and rolling hills, as well as challenging ascents into the imposing Sierra de Bernia and Sierra de Oltá mountain ranges. The region also features scenic coastal rides along the Mediterranean, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Benissa caters to all levels. While many routes feature significant climbs, there are also easier options. The region's diverse topography includes gentle country roads and coastal paths, offering a more relaxed cycling experience. Out of the 19 routes available, 4 are classified as easy and 7 as moderate, providing plenty of choices for less experienced riders.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in Benissa?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Benissa offers several difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32539776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Vall de Pop – Coll de Rates loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt; includes the well-known Coll de Rates climb. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15208118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall d'Ebo Pass – Pego–Sagra Road loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses inland passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890646615/montgo-and-la-vall-de-laguar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgó and La Vall de Laguar Loop&lt;/a&gt; also presents a significant challenge, leading through the prominent Montgó massif.

Q: What kind of scenic views and landmarks can I expect along the routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Benissa offer breathtaking scenery. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Mediterranean coastline, rugged mountain ranges like Sierra de Bernia, and serene inland landscapes adorned with vineyards. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/889730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Salinas de Calpe&lt;/a&gt;, where you might spot flamingos, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4071030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mascarat Canyon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Benissa?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Benissa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32539776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Vall de Pop – Coll de Rates loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15208118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall d'Ebo Pass – Pego–Sagra Road loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes provide a convenient way to explore the diverse landscapes of the region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Benissa?
A: Benissa is often described as a 'cycling paradise' due to its privileged climate, making it suitable for road cycling year-round. The mild Mediterranean climate means pleasant temperatures for most of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable conditions, with less heat than summer and milder weather than winter, though winter cycling is also popular among professionals and enthusiasts.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Benissa?
A: While many routes involve significant climbs, the region does offer gentler sections suitable for families. The Benissa coastline features a well-marked coastal trail, the Paseo Ecológico, which, while not a dedicated road cycling route, provides a safe and scenic option for leisurely rides. For road cycling specifically, look for the 'easy' or 'moderate' classified routes, which tend to have less elevation gain and are often on well-maintained, quieter roads.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Benissa?
A: The road cycling experience in Benissa is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse terrain, which offers everything from challenging mountain climbs to scenic coastal rides. The well-maintained roads, cycle-friendly environment, and breathtaking Mediterranean landscapes are also commonly highlighted as major draws.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the routes?
A: Yes, Benissa and its surrounding villages are well-equipped with cyclist-friendly cafes and establishments. Many routes, especially those passing through charming inland villages like Xaló, offer opportunities to stop for refreshments. The region's popularity with cyclists means that many local businesses cater to their needs, providing convenient breaks during your ride.

Q: How well-maintained are the roads for cycling in Benissa?
A: The roads in Benissa and its hinterland are generally very well-maintained, featuring smooth asphalt. Local authorities actively ensure the quality of the road network, recognizing the positive economic impact of cycling tourism. This makes for a comfortable and enjoyable riding experience, attracting both amateur and professional cyclists to the area.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists near the starting points of routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Benissa and the surrounding towns, often near the starting points of popular cycling routes. As cycling is a significant activity in the region, local infrastructure usually accommodates vehicles for those transporting their bikes. It's advisable to check specific parking options for your chosen starting location.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in Benissa?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads in Benissa or the wider Costa Blanca region. Cyclists are expected to adhere to standard Spanish traffic laws. If you plan to venture into specific natural parks or protected areas, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations, though this is less common for road cycling than for mountain biking or hiking.
tips:
The road connects the cities of Denia and Jávea. It is a very beautiful climb with views of the sea and the mountains. The highest peak on the road is in the La Plana area. Here, there is a car park from where trails lead up to the top of the mountain.

The pavement of the road is in perfect condition. There is hardly any shoulder on the road and you have to be careful with the traffic, especially in the busiest months of the year.

The ascent from Sagra is done along the provincial road, it is quite calm. Halfway up the climb, in the town of Benimaurell, the road becomes a paved road. Above all, the locals use it to move to their crops. There are steps to overtaking. The last slopes are very steep, save your energy for when you get to this section.

At the end of the climb, there is a restaurant on the right hand side. It is an ideal place to try typical foods of the mountains.

An obligatory stop when you go out for a ride on the roads of the Marina Alta. A place with everything you need, coffee and bar with a very good offer and quality, a workshop to repair any emergency and a very friendly treatment of all the staff. A highly recommended site!

faqs.question: How challenging is the Montgó and La Vall de Laguar Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a difficult road ride, requiring very good fitness. It covers approximately 92.8 km with about 1,347 meters of elevation gain. You'll encounter irregular climbs, including steep sections up to 14%, and winding mountain roads.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily well-paved, with roads around Montgó generally in perfect condition. In La Vall de Laguar, roads between villages like Fleix and Benimaurell have been recently re-asphalted, offering excellent surfaces. However, expect winding and narrow mountain roads with significant climbs and descents.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: While the tour is circular from Benissa, specific parking details are not provided. It's advisable to look for public parking in Benissa or other nearby towns like Dénia or Jávea, depending on your preferred starting point. Ensure parking is suitable for leaving a vehicle for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The mild weather from autumn to spring makes this region a popular training ground for cyclists. Summers can be very hot, so spring and autumn offer more comfortable temperatures for tackling the challenging climbs.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in Parc Natural del Montgó?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling through Parc Natural del Montgó. However, as it's a natural park, always respect local regulations regarding environmental protection and road safety.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the Montgó and La Vall de Laguar Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking views throughout the ride. Key highlights include the challenging Ascent to Montgó from Dénia, offering vistas of the sea and mountains, and the Steep climb to Venta Del Collao/Back way to Vall de Laguar. La Vall de Laguar is known for its ancient Moorish farming terraces and seasonal waterfalls. On clear days, you might even see Ibiza from higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by the Velosol Cycling Cafe, a popular spot for cyclists. Additionally, you'll pass through picturesque villages in La Vall de Laguar where you can find local cafes and restaurants to refuel and rest.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this loop overlap with several other official routes. Notably, it covers parts of La Vall de Laguar-La Vuelta al Cavall Verd (19%), [CIMA AT13] La Garga * Sagra (12%), [CIMA AT13] La Garga * Orba (10%), and El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana (10%).

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route designed for racebikes. It is not suitable for dogs to accompany you on the bike. If you are traveling with a dog, they would need to be in a support vehicle, but the focus of this tour is on the cycling experience.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, bring plenty of water, energy gels or snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear. Sun protection is essential, especially during sunnier months. A GPS device with the route loaded is highly recommended due to the winding mountain roads.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the route that are particularly busy or require extra caution?
faqs.answer: Roads around Montgó can be busy, especially during peak months, and often lack a wide shoulder. Always be vigilant of traffic, particularly on descents and winding sections. Some climbs in La Vall de Laguar are very steep, up to 14%, so ensure your bike is in good condition and you are prepared for the effort.

waypoints.name: Steep climb to Venta Del Collao/Back way to Vall de Laguar
